name: HMAS ML 826 (ML 826)
events: 7 Oct 1944 HMS Sirdar (Lt. J.A. Spender, RN) departed Fremantle for her 6th war patrol (4th in the Far East and 1st in the South-West Pacific area). She was ordered to patrol in the Sunda Strait and on the way back to Fremantle to reconnoitre Christmas Island. She was escorted out by HMAS ML 826.
For the daily positions of HMS Sirdar during this patrol see the map below.

21 Dec 1944 During 21/22 December 1944, USS Besugo (T/Cdr. T.L. Wogan, USN), USS Blackfin (T/Lt.Cdr. C.L. Raguet, USN) and USS Hardhead (T/Cdr. F.A. Greenup, USN) conducted convoy attack exercises off Fremantle. These included night exercises.

The simulated convoy was made up of the frigate USS Corpus Christie (Cdr. W.W. Childress, USCG) and the submarine rescue vessel USS Coucal (T/Lt. W.P. Baron, USN). They were escorted by the minesweepers HMAS Dubbo (T/Lt. F.W. Roberts, RANR(S)), HMAS Inverell (Lt. A.I. Chapman, RANR(S)), HMAS Mildura (Lt.Cdr. J.M. Little, RANR(S)), HMAS Warrnambool (Lt.Cdr.(Emgy.) E.J.B. Wight, RAN), armed yacht USS Isabel (Lt. E.G. Zacher, USNR), motor minesweepers USS YMS 11 (Lt. D.C. Maughan, USNR), USS YMS 72 (Lt. J.E. Reilly, USNR) and motor launches HMAS ML 815 (Lt. A.C. McAllister, RANVR) and HMAS ML 826 (Lt. S.A. Hancock, RANR).
Before commencement of the night exercises both motor minesweepers and both motor launches parted company. (2)

28 Apr 1945 Around 1200 I/28, HrMs O 24 (Lt.Cdr. P.J.S. de Jong, RNN) ended her 23th war patrol (11th in the Far East and 1st for this deployment) at Fremantle. On entering harbour D/G trials were carried out.
She was escorted in by HMAS ML 826 (Lt. S.A. Hancock, RANR) which had joined around 0930 H/28. (3)
18 Jun 1945 Around 1600 I/18, HrMs O 24 (Lt.Cdr. P.J.S. de Jong, RNN) arrived at Darwin.
She was escorted in by HMAS ML 807 (Lt. W.M. Marley, DSC, RANVR) which had joined around 1245 I/18. (3)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
